description_long: This object is a compact audio cassette tape, featuring a rectangular plastic shell with two visible spools for magnetic tape. The transparent casing allows a view of the internal tape reels, which are wound around yellow hubs. The front label displays branding and technical information, including the BASF logo and tape type. The cassette has a series of small openings and notches along its bottom edge, which are used for tape deck alignment and write-protection. The overall design is utilitarian, with a mix of clear and dark plastic, and the tape window provides a glimpse of the magnetic tape inside. This type of cassette was commonly used for audio recording and playback in portable and home stereo systems.
description: A transparent plastic audio cassette tape with visible yellow spools and BASF branding, showing internal tape reels and technical labeling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the BASF label, both yellow tape spools, and the main tape window.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cassette's thickness and the clear plastic shell.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the opposite side of the cassette, with less visible labeling and the back of the spools.
description_view_3: Angled side view from the opposite end, again showing the cassette's thickness and the clear plastic shell.
